description: Decimal fixed-point and floating-point arithmetic. Provides support for fast correctly-rounded decimal floating-point arithmetic.

functions.name: Decimal

params.name: value
params.description: The value to convert. Can be an integer, a string (e.g. '3.14', 'Infinity', 'NaN'), a tuple (sign, digits, exponent), or a float. If omitted, returns Decimal('0').

params.name: context
params.description: Optional context for the conversion. If not provided, the current thread's context is used.

returns.description: A new Decimal object

functions.description: Constructs a new Decimal object from value. Preferred over float for financial and precision-sensitive calculations. Strings are the recommended way to construct Decimals to avoid float precision issues.

functions.name: getcontext

returns.description: The current Context object for the active thread

functions.description: Returns the current context for the active thread. This context controls precision, rounding, traps, and flags for decimal operations.

functions.name: setcontext

params.name: c
params.description: The Context object to set as the current context

returns.description: No return value

functions.description: Sets the current context for the active thread to c.

functions.name: localcontext

params.name: c
params.description: Optional context to use. If not provided, a copy of the current context is used.

returns.description: A context manager that sets the active decimal context to a copy of c on entry and restores the previous context on exit

functions.description: Returns a context manager that sets the decimal context to a copy of c (or the current context if not given) for the duration of the with block, then restores the previous context.

classes.name: Decimal
classes.description: Represents a decimal floating-point number. Provides exact decimal arithmetic unlike binary floats. Suitable for financial calculations and anywhere exact decimal representation is needed.

params.name: value
params.description: An integer, string, tuple (sign, digit_tuple, exponent), or float. Strings like '3.14', '-0.001', 'Infinity', 'NaN' are supported.

constructors.description: Creates a new Decimal from the given value. Use strings for exact representation: Decimal('0.1') is exact while Decimal(0.1) inherits float imprecision.

methods.name: adjusted

returns.description: The adjusted exponent

methods.description: Returns the adjusted exponent after shifting the coefficient's rightmost digits until only the lead digit remains. Decimal('321e+5').adjusted() returns 7.

methods.name: as_tuple

returns.description: A named tuple with fields sign, digits, and exponent

methods.description: Returns the number as a named tuple DecimalTuple(sign, digits, exponent). sign is 0 for positive, 1 for negative. digits is a tuple of ints.

methods.name: canonical

returns.description: The canonical encoding of the Decimal

methods.description: Returns the canonical encoding of the argument. Currently, the encoding of a Decimal is always canonical, so this returns its argument unchanged.

methods.name: compare

returns.description: Decimal('-1') if self < other, Decimal('0') if equal, Decimal('1') if self > other

methods.description: Compares two Decimal values numerically. Returns Decimal('-1'), Decimal('0'), or Decimal('1') for less than, equal, or greater than respectively. NaN signals InvalidOperation.

methods.name: conjugate

returns.description: Returns self (Decimals are real numbers)

methods.description: Returns self. This method is only to comply with the Decimal Specification.

methods.name: copy_abs

returns.description: The absolute value of self

methods.description: Returns the absolute value of the operand. This operation is unaffected by context and is quiet (no flags are changed).

methods.name: copy_negate

returns.description: The negation of self

methods.description: Returns the negation of the operand. This operation is unaffected by context and is quiet.

methods.name: copy_sign

params.name: other
params.description: The Decimal whose sign to copy

returns.description: A copy of self with the sign of other

methods.description: Returns a copy of the first operand with the sign set to be the same as the sign of the second operand.

methods.name: exp

returns.description: e**self

methods.description: Returns the value of the (natural) exponential function e**x at the given number.

methods.name: fma

returns.description: self * other + third with no rounding of the intermediate product

methods.description: Fused multiply-add. Returns self*other+third with no rounding of the intermediate product self*other.

methods.name: is_finite

returns.description: True if the Decimal is finite (not infinity or NaN)

methods.description: Returns True if the argument is a finite number, and False if the argument is an infinity or a NaN.

methods.name: is_infinite

returns.description: True if the Decimal is infinite

methods.description: Returns True if the argument is either positive or negative infinity, and False otherwise.

methods.name: is_nan

returns.description: True if the Decimal is NaN (quiet or signaling)

methods.description: Returns True if the argument is a (quiet or signaling) NaN and False otherwise.

methods.name: is_normal

returns.description: True if the Decimal is a normal finite number

methods.description: Returns True if the argument is a normal finite number. Returns False if the argument is zero, subnormal, infinite, or NaN.

methods.name: is_signed

returns.description: True if the Decimal has a negative sign

methods.description: Returns True if the argument has a negative sign and False otherwise. Note that zeros and NaNs can both carry signs.

methods.name: is_zero

returns.description: True if the Decimal is zero

methods.description: Returns True if the argument is a (positive or negative) zero and False otherwise.

methods.name: ln

returns.description: The natural (base e) logarithm of self

methods.description: Returns the natural (base e) logarithm of the operand. The result is correctly rounded using the ROUND_HALF_EVEN rounding mode.

methods.name: log10

returns.description: The base-10 logarithm of self

methods.description: Returns the base-10 logarithm of the operand. The result is correctly rounded using the ROUND_HALF_EVEN rounding mode.

methods.name: logb

returns.description: The adjusted exponent of self

methods.description: For a non-zero number, returns the adjusted exponent of the operand as a Decimal instance. Equivalent to Decimal(self.adjusted()).

methods.name: max

returns.description: The larger of self and other

methods.description: Like max(self, other) except that the context rounding rule is applied before returning and that NaN values are either signaled or ignored.

methods.name: min

returns.description: The smaller of self and other

methods.description: Like min(self, other) except that the context rounding rule is applied before returning and that NaN values are either signaled or ignored.

methods.name: normalize

returns.description: The simplest form of self with trailing zeros removed

methods.description: Normalizes the number by stripping the rightmost trailing zeros and converting any result equal to Decimal('0') to Decimal('0e0'). Used to produce canonical values for comparison.

methods.name: quantize

params.name: exp
params.description: The exponent to quantize to (e.g., Decimal('0.01') for 2 decimal places)

params.name: rounding
params.description: Rounding mode. If omitted, uses the context's rounding.

params.name: watchexp
params.description: If True (default), an error is returned when the resulting exponent is greater than Emax or less than Etiny.

returns.description: A value equal to self (rounded) with the exponent of exp

methods.description: Returns a value equal to the first operand after rounding and having the exponent of exp. Useful for monetary applications: Decimal('7.325').quantize(Decimal('.01'), rounding=ROUND_DOWN) gives Decimal('7.32').

methods.name: remainder_near

returns.description: self - n * other where n is the nearest integer to self / other

methods.description: Computes the modulo such that the remainder has the same sign as the divisor. Returns self - n * other where n is the integer nearest to the exact value of self / other.

methods.name: rotate

params.name: other
params.description: The number of places to rotate (integer)

returns.description: The rotated result

methods.description: Returns the result of rotating the digits of the first operand by an amount specified by the second operand. Positive other rotates left, negative rotates right.

methods.name: same_quantum

params.name: other
params.description: The Decimal to compare quantum with

returns.description: True if both operands have the same exponent

methods.description: Returns True if the two operands have the same exponent. Tests whether Decimal('1.00') and Decimal('1.0') have the same quantum (they do not).

methods.name: scaleb

returns.description: self with exponent adjusted by other

methods.description: Returns the first operand with the exponent adjusted by the second. Equivalent to multiplying by 10**other.

methods.name: shift

returns.description: The shifted result

methods.description: Returns the result of shifting the digits of the first operand by an amount specified by the second operand. Positive shifts left (multiplies by power of 10), negative shifts right. Digits shifted in are zeros.

methods.name: sqrt

returns.description: The square root of self

methods.description: Returns the square root of the argument to full precision.

methods.name: to_eng_string

returns.description: A string in engineering notation

methods.description: Converts to a string, using engineering notation if an exponent is needed. Engineering notation has an exponent that is a multiple of 3.

methods.name: to_integral_value

params.name: rounding
params.description: Rounding mode. If omitted, uses the context's rounding.

returns.description: The nearest integer Decimal value

methods.description: Rounds to the nearest integer without signaling Inexact or Rounded. If the operand is already an integer, the result is unchanged.

classes.name: Context
classes.description: Represents the arithmetic context for Decimal operations. Controls precision, rounding, limits, and trap/flag behavior. Each thread has its own current context accessible via getcontext().

constructors.signature: Context(prec=None, rounding=None, traps=None, flags=None, Emin=None, Emax=None, capitals=1)

params.name: prec
params.description: Precision (number of significant digits). Default is 28.

params.name: rounding
params.description: Rounding mode (e.g. ROUND_HALF_EVEN). Default is ROUND_HALF_EVEN.

params.name: traps
params.description: Dictionary of signal-to-bool mappings indicating which signals are trapped

params.name: flags
params.description: Dictionary of signal-to-bool mappings indicating which flags are set

params.name: capitals
params.description: If 1 (default), use 'E' in string representations. If 0, use 'e'.

constructors.description: Creates a new Context with the specified settings. Any omitted fields use defaults from the DefaultContext.

methods.name: copy

returns.description: A duplicate of the context

methods.description: Returns a duplicate of the context.

methods.name: create_decimal

params.name: num
params.description: The value to convert. Creates a Decimal using the context's settings.

returns.description: A new Decimal created with this context's settings

methods.description: Creates a new Decimal instance from num but using the context's settings for rounding. Unlike the Decimal constructor, the context precision, rounding, and flags are applied to the conversion.

methods.name: clear_flags

returns.description: No return value

methods.description: Resets all flags to zero.

fields.name: prec
fields.description: The precision for arithmetic operations (number of significant digits)

fields.name: rounding
fields.description: The rounding mode (one of the ROUND_* constants)

fields.name: capitals
fields.description: If 1, exponents are printed with a capital E; otherwise, lowercase e

fields.name: flags
fields.description: Dictionary mapping signals to booleans, recording which signals have occurred during computation

fields.name: traps
fields.description: Dictionary mapping signals to booleans, determining which signals are converted into exceptions

constants.name: ROUND_HALF_DOWN
constants.description: Round to nearest with ties going towards zero.

constants.name: ROUND_HALF_EVEN
constants.description: Round to nearest with ties going to nearest even integer (banker's rounding). This is the default rounding mode.

constants.name: ROUND_HALF_UP
constants.description: Round to nearest with ties going away from zero.

constants.name: ROUND_05UP
constants.description: Round away from zero if the last digit after rounding towards zero would have been 0 or 5; otherwise round towards zero.

constants.name: Infinity
constants.description: Shorthand for Decimal('Infinity'). Represents positive infinity.

constants.name: NegativeInfinity
constants.description: Shorthand for Decimal('-Infinity'). Represents negative infinity.

constants.name: NaN
constants.description: Shorthand for Decimal('NaN'). Represents a quiet Not-a-Number.
